subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The Hierarchy of Controls
description: Learn The Hierarchy of Controls in one minute.

hook.text: Telling workers to 'be more careful' is the weakest safety measure. Engineers know a better way.

buildup.text: The hierarchy of controls ranks safety measures from most to least effective.

discovery.text: Elimination is best. Then substitution, engineering controls, admin controls, and finally PPE.

twist.text: Most companies start at the bottom—giving workers hard hats instead of removing the falling hazard.

climax.text: Working up the hierarchy costs more upfront but prevents more injuries per dollar spent.

punchline.text: Remove the hazard. Don't just protect the worker standing next to it.

quiz.question: What is the most effective level in the hierarchy of controls?
